hero_subtitle: SFTY structures suspicious transaction reporting by linking wallet activity, risk signals, VASP data, and evidence into an auditable environment tailored for FIU investigations.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_description: FIU teams struggle to assemble and correlate fragmented wallet activity, risk signals and case evidence into coherent SAR and STR reports. This disjointed workflow slows investigation readiness and complicates regulatory reporting obligations for digital asset cases.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects wallet activity, VASP profiles, risk signals, and case evidence into one structured workflow that supports FIU teams in streamlined suspicious activity investigation and report preparation.
workflow_title: From suspicious activity to reporting evidence
workflow_description: SFTY connects wallet and transaction risk signals to case creation, evidence collection, supervisory review and regulatory reporting for FIU workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervision layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects wallet activity, VASP data, cases, evidence and reporting into a unified environment designed for FIU workflows and crypto SAR/STR supervision.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY supports regulators with secure deployment, controlled access and evidence-grade logging to support structured, defensible workflows for AML intelligence and reporting.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY about structuring crypto SAR and STR reporting
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how suspicious activity flows into FIU case workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports crypto SAR reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe multi-provider orchestration for regulatory reporting workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for FIUs and AML authorities?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides FIUs and AML authorities with a connected platform to connect digital asset data, risk signals, cases and reporting into a structured, evidence-ready supervisory operating layer.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support suspicious activity workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures suspicious activity data into alerts, linked cases, evidence packages and reports, supporting regulated workflows from initial detection through investigation and SAR/STR-style output preparation.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready reporting evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs, entity linkages and supporting transaction data to support supervisory teams can review and defend reporting outcomes effectively.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing supervisory systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, existing supervisory platforms and multi-provider data feeds within a secure, access-controlled deployment.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing analytics or compliance tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates as a supervisory layer that connects and organizes outputs from multiple analytics and compliance providers without replacing the underlying tools.
final_description: Review how SFTY connects wallet, VASP and risk data into audit-ready cases and evidence for FIU AML intelligence workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
